titles.text: A Randomized, Open_Label, Phase 3 Study of Abemaciclib Combined with Standard Adjuvant Endocrine Therapy versus Standard Adjuvant Endocrine Therapy Alone in Patients with High Risk, Node Positive, Early Stage, Hormone Receptor Positive, Human Epidermal Receptor 2 Negative, Breast Cancer

abbreviations.expandedText: Functional Assessment of Cancer Therapy _ Endocrine Symptoms (Version 4)
Eastern Cooperative Oncology Group performance status
human epidermal growth factor receptor 2 negative

eligibilityCriterionItems.name: Age and Sex Requirement
eligibilityCriterionItems.text: Female (regardless of menopausal status) or male ≥18 years of age (or of an acceptable age according to local regulations, whichever is older).

eligibilityCriterionItems.name: Confirmed HR+, HER2_negative, early stage resected invasive breast cancer
eligibilityCriterionItems.text:
The patient has confirmed HR+, HER2_negative (HER2_), early stage resected invasive breast cancer without evidence of distant metastases.
 To fulfill the requirement for HR+ disease by local testing on primary disease specimen, tumor must be ER or PgR positive defined by immunohistochemistry (IHC) according to American Society of Clinical Oncology (ASCO)/College of American Pathologists (CAP) guidelines for hormone receptor testing (Hammond et al. 2010).
 To fulfill the requirement of HER2_ disease by local testing on primary disease specimen, tumor must be HER2_ according to ASCO/CAP guidelines for HER2 testing (Wolff et al. 2018).
Patients with bilateral breast cancer (diagnosis of invasive tumors in both breasts simultaneously or within 6 months of each other) can be eligible if all lesions tested on both sides are HR+/HER2_ and adequate surgery has been performed in both breasts (see inclusion criterion [3]). The Lilly Clinical Research Physician and Clinical Research Scientist (CRP/CRS) must be consulted for all cases of bilateral breast cancer.

eligibilityCriterionItems.name: Definitive surgery of primary breast tumor(
eligibilityCriterionItems.text:
The patient must have undergone definitive surgery of the primary breast tumor(s).
 With the exception of the situations described below, the margins of the resected specimen must be histologically free of invasive tumor and /or a component of ductal carcinoma in situ (DCIS) as determined by the local pathologist. If pathologic examination demonstrates tumor at the line of resection, additional excisions may be performed to obtain clear margins. If tumor is still present at the resected margin after re_excision(s), the patient must undergo mastectomy to be eligible. Of note, patients with margins positive for lobular carcinoma in situ (LCIS) are eligible without additional resection.
 For patients who undergo mastectomy or wide local excision where deep margin abuts the pectoralis fascia, patients with microscopic positive margins are eligible as long as radiotherapy of the chest wall is administered prior to study entry. Patients with positive anterior margins may be eligible if there is no gross disease left behind (radiotherapy as per local guidelines).
 Where surgical excision of supraclavicular or internal mammary nodes is not feasible, residual nodes should be irradiated in accordance with standard guidelines.
 If given, radiation therapy (for example, post_mastectomy or post_lumpectomy) should be administered according to standard guidelines.

eligibilityCriterionItems.name: Tumor tissue for exploratory biomarker analysis
eligibilityCriterionItems.text:
The patient must have tumor tissue from breast (preferred) or lymph node for exploratory biomarker analysis available prior to randomization. 
Note: Sites should confirm the availability of tumor tissue for exploratory analysis (Section 9.8.2.2) with the pathological laboratory prior to randomization

eligibilityCriterionItems.name: Node positive status and additional criteria
eligibilityCriterionItems.text:
Patients must be node positive (microscopic and macroscopic tumor involvement are allowed; ipsilateral internal mammary and supraclavicular lymph nodes are allowed, but will not count toward the number of positive lymph nodes) and fulfill one of the following criteria: 
A. Pathological tumor involvement in ≥4 ipsilateral axillary lymph nodes.
OR
B. Pathological tumor involvement in 1 to 3 ipsilateral axillary lymph node(s) (for patients who received neoadjuvant therapy also cytological tumor involvement at time of initial diagnosis is allowed) and meet at least 1 of the following criteria:
1. Grade 3 as defined by a combined score of at least 8 points per the modified Bloom_Richardson grading system (Elston and Ellis 1991), also known as the Nottingham scale, or equivalent following discussion with the Lilly CRP/CRS
– pathological primary invasive tumor size ≥5 cm (for patients who received neoadjuvant therapy primary tumor size ≥5 cm on breast imaging is allowed). Note: if tumor size is needed to meet eligibility criteria, patients with multifocal/multicentric tumors may be eligible based on the addition of diameters of the individual lesions following discussion with the Lilly CRP/CRS.
– Ki_67 index of ≥20% (for Cohort 2) on untreated breast tissue as determined by the investigational assay (described in Section 3.2.1) at the Study JPCF central laboratory. See Section 9.8.1 for Ki_67 sample requirements.

arms.name: Abemaciclib + Endocrine Therapy (Arm A)
arms.description: Patients receive abemaciclib orally at 150 mg twice daily for up to 2 years in combination with standard adjuvant endocrine therapy of physician’s choice.

arms.name: Endocrine Therapy Alone (Arm B)
arms.description: Patients receive standard adjuvant endocrine therapy of physician’s choice alone for at least 5 years.

objectives.name: Primary Efficacy Objective
objectives.text: To evaluate the efficacy, in terms of IDFS, for patients with HR+, HER2_ early stage breast cancer for abemaciclib plus adjuvant endocrine therapy versus adjuvant endocrine therapy alone.

endpoints.name: Primary Efficacy Endpoint (IDFS)
endpoints.text: IDFS, as defined by the STEEP System (Hudis et al. 2007) (Section 10.3.1.1)

objectives.name: Secondary Efficacy Objective (Ki_67 subgrou
objectives.text: To evaluate the efficacy, in terms of IDFS, for patients with HR+, HER2_ early stage breast cancer with pretreatment Ki_67 index ≥20% by central lab

endpoints.name: Secondary Efficacy Endpoint (IDFS in Ki_67 subgrou
endpoints.text: IDFS, as defined by the STEEP System

endpoints.name: Secondary Quality of Life Endpoint (EQ_5D_5L)
endpoints.text: The EQ_5D_5L health state profile (the index score and the single_item health status measure)

objectives.name: Secondary Efficacy Objective (DRFS and OS)
objectives.text: To evaluate the efficacy of abemaciclib plus adjuvant endocrine therapy versus adjuvant endocrine therapy alone in terms of DRFS and OS

endpoints.name: Secondary Safety Endpoint (Vital signs and physical examination
Secondary Quality of Life Endpoint (FACT_B)
endpoints.text: FACT_B questionnaire for general oncology and breast cancer health_related quality of life

objectives.name: Secondary Safety Objective
objectives.text: To assess the safety profile of abemaciclib plus adjuvant endocrine therapy compared to adjuvant endocrine therapy alone

objectives.name: Secondary Pharmacokinetic/Clinical Outcomes Relationship Objective
objectives.text: To evaluate the relationship between abemaciclib, exposure and clinical (efficacy and safety) outcomes

endpoints.name: Secondary Efficacy Endpoint (Hazard ratio for IDFS)
Secondary Quality of Life Endpoint (FACIT_sourced cognitive symptom
endpoints.text: additional FACIT_sourced items for cognitive symptoms

objectives.name: Secondary Quality of Life Objective
objectives.text: To evaluate abemaciclib plus adjuvant endocrine therapy, versus adjuvant endocrine therapy alone, in terms of general oncology and breast cancer self_reported health_related quality of life (FACT_B 37_item questionnaire), endocrine therapy_specific symptoms (the FACT_ES 19_item subscale and 2 FACIT_sourced items of cognitive symptoms and 3 FACIT_sourced items for bladder symptoms), and fatigue experienced during abemaciclib and/or endocrine therapy (the FACIT_F 13_item subscale).

endpoints.name: Secondary Efficacy Endpoint (Hazard ratio for DRFS)
Secondary Quality of Life Endpoint (FACIT_sourced bladder symptom
endpoints.text: additional FACIT_sourced items for bladder symptoms

objectives.name: Secondary Health Status/Economic Evaluation Objective
objectives.text: To evaluate health status to inform decision modeling for health economic evaluation using the EQ_5D_5L.

endpoints.name: Secondary Efficacy Endpoint (Hazard ratio for OS)

notes.text: The information contained in this protocol is confidential and is intended for the use of clinical investigators. It is the property of Eli Lilly and Company or its subsidiaries and should not be copied by or distributed to persons not involved in the clinical investigation of abemaciclib (LY2835219), unless such persons are bound by a confidentiality agreement with Eli Lilly and Company or its subsidiaries.

This document may contain protected personal data and/or commercially confidential information exempt from public disclosure. Eli Lilly and Company requests consultation regarding release/redaction prior to any public release. In the United States, this document is subject to Freedom of Information Act (FOIA) Exemption 4 and may not be reproduced or otherwise disseminated without the written approval of Eli Lilly and Company or its subsidiaries.
Use of GNRH analogues for ovarian suppression is not considered endocrine therapy for the purposes of this criterion. Note: Adjuvant treatment with fulvestrant is not allowed.

studyInterventions.name: Abemaciclib
studyInterventions.description: Investigational product for breast cancer, combined with standard adjuvant endocrine therapy.

studyInterventions.name: Standard Adjuvant Endocrine Therapy
studyInterventions.description: Standard of care endocrine therapy of physician’s choice, given alone or in combination with abemaciclib.

studyInterventions.name: Tamoxifen
studyInterventions.description: An example of standard adjuvant endocrine therapy.

studyInterventions.name: Aromatase Inhibitor
studyInterventions.description: A class of drugs used as standard adjuvant endocrine therapy.

studyInterventions.name: Letrozole
studyInterventions.description: An example of an aromatase inhibitor.

studyInterventions.name: Anastrozole
studyInterventions.description: An example of an aromatase inhibitor.

studyInterventions.name: Exemestane
studyInterventions.description: An example of an aromatase inhibitor.

studyInterventions.name: GnRH Agonist
studyInterventions.description: Used for ovarian function suppression as part of standard adjuvant endocrine therapy.

studyInterventions.name: Fulvestrant
studyInterventions.description: Adjuvant treatment with fulvestrant is not allowed at any time during the study.

studyInterventions.name: Any CDK4 and CDK6 inhibitor
studyInterventions.description: Previous treatment with any CDK4 and CDK6 inhibitor is an exclusion criterion.

studyInterventions.name: Exogenous reproductive hormone therapy
studyInterventions.description: Concurrent use of exogenous reproductive hormone therapy (e.g., birth control pills, hormone replacement therapy) is prohibited.

studyInterventions.name: Birth control pills
studyInterventions.description: An example of exogenous reproductive hormone therapy.

studyInterventions.name: Hormone replacement therapy
studyInterventions.description: An example of exogenous reproductive hormone therapy.

studyInterventions.name: Megestrol acetate
studyInterventions.description: Megestrol acetate is not permitted.

studyInterventions.name: Endocrine therapy for breast cancer prevention
studyInterventions.description: Previous use of endocrine therapy for breast cancer prevention (e.g., tamoxifen or aromatase inhibitors) or raloxifene is an exclusion criterion.

studyInterventions.name: Raloxifene
studyInterventions.description: Previous use of raloxifene for breast cancer prevention is an exclusion criterion.

studyInterventions.name: Other anticancer therapy
studyInterventions.description: No other anticancer therapy will be permitted while patients are on study treatment, with the exception of standard endocrine therapy for breast cancer.

studyInterventions.name: Strong CYP3A inhibitors
studyInterventions.description: Should be substituted or avoided if possible due to potential drug interaction with abemaciclib.

studyInterventions.name: Clarithromycin
studyInterventions.description: An example of a strong CYP3A inhibitor that should be substituted or avoided.

studyInterventions.name: Strong CYP3A inducers
studyInterventions.description: Should be substituted or avoided if possible due to potential drug interaction with abemaciclib.

studyInterventions.name: Rifampin
studyInterventions.description: An example of a strong CYP3A inducer that should be substituted or avoided.

studyInterventions.name: Substrates of P_glycoprotein, BCRP, OAT2, MATE1, MATE2_K with narrow therapeutic index
studyInterventions.description: Should be substituted or avoided if possible due to potential drug interaction with abemaciclib.

studyInterventions.name: Metformin
studyInterventions.description: An example of a transporter substrate with narrow therapeutic index that should be substituted or avoided.

studyInterventions.name: Digoxin
studyInterventions.description: An example of a transporter substrate with narrow therapeutic index that should be substituted or avoided.

studyInterventions.name: Dofetilide
studyInterventions.description: An example of a transporter substrate with narrow therapeutic index that should be substituted or avoided.

studyInterventions.name: Standard of care bone_modifying agents
studyInterventions.description: Concurrent treatment with agents such as bisphosphonates and denosumab is permitted.

studyInterventions.name: Bisphosphonates
studyInterventions.description: An example of a standard of care bone_modifying agent.

studyInterventions.name: Denosumab
studyInterventions.description: An example of a standard of care bone_modifying agent.

studyInterventions.name: Topical vaginal estrogen therapy
studyInterventions.description: Permitted if all other non_hormonal options are exhausted.

studyInterventions.name: Antiemetics
studyInterventions.description: Permitted for supportive care to maximize quality of life.

studyInterventions.name: Growth factors
studyInterventions.description: May be administered in accordance with ASCO guidelines for neutropenia, with specific abemaciclib dose adjustments.

studyInterventions.name: Antidiarrheal therapy
studyInterventions.description: Should be initiated as early as possible for diarrhea management.

studyInterventions.name: Loperamide
studyInterventions.description: An example of antidiarrheal therapy.

studyInterventions.name: Broad_spectrum antibiotics
studyInterventions.description: Must be prescribed for severe diarrhea or any grade of diarrhea associated with fever or severe neutropenia.

studyInterventions.name: Fluoroquinolones
studyInterventions.description: An example of broad_spectrum antibiotics.

studyInterventions.name: IV fluid
studyInterventions.description: Administered for severe diarrhea or any grade of diarrhea associated with severe nausea or vomiting.

studyInterventions.name: Electrolyte replacement
studyInterventions.description: Administered for severe diarrhea or any grade of diarrhea associated with severe nausea or vomiting.

studyInterventions.name: IV antibiotic therapy
studyInterventions.description: Urgent initiation for febrile neutropenia.
